Tax Sale – Swipe Files
Introduction to the Tax Sale
The Quick Launch is very cool because it’s a way to get money quickly. There is very little
work, it’s very quick, there are often no Prelaunch Content pieces whatsoever; you just need
some type of a story (and it doesn’t have to be a fantastic story… this Tax Sale was a pretty
simple story.)
After you have your story, it’s not all that much more than clicking the button and you get
money. What could be better than that, right?
So there are a couple of different ways to do this and you’ll see that in the examples. Basically
you’ve got your list and some type of an offer. You then have some type of a story.
One of the examples I’m going to show you is one that I’ve been teaching for several years now.
I keep teaching it because it’s a great example and my Product Launch Formula owners keep
on using it and it keeps on working. That’s my famous “Tax Sale”.
The Tax Sale
In this case, as the story goes, I got a nasty tax surprise and it was like, “Holy cow, I have
this giant tax bill. What am I going to do?” I’d rather bring in some more money to pay
for this tax bill than like write a check for it. That’s literally what I told the people on my list
was like, “Hey, you know I never discount this product, but I need to bring in
money because I had a big tax bill. And so for the next day and a half it’s going
to be on sale until April 16th, the day after tax day.”
So what I did was I created that story and that was my little quick launch story and it drove
into the offer. It was so quick the line was only a third that long. We had the offer and literally
this was day one. I came up with the offer and I nailed that offer. I put together a short sales
letter, I mailed people and I said, “Hey, I’ve got this tax bill. It’s April 14th or April 15
and it’s going to be on sale for the next two days.” So I sent them to the offer with this
story.
The story allowed me to discount my product without having to reduce the perceived value. So
then what I did was I followed it up by sending an email. The email sent them to the offer and
then I sent email number two and three. It was the offer, you’ve got two days, one day, the last
day and it’s done. It was that simple.
The whole thing, especially with the story, was just telling it to my list. I repeated the story,
gave them a hard deadline and out here we had a deadline, “Buy by midnight.” I think my
story was, “Buy by midnight on April 16th. I’m mailing my check on April 15th. I
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Email #1
==== MESSAGE #1 =====
Sent early in the morning on April 14th.
----- begin message -----
Subject: RTU - "Jeff's huge tax bill"
OK, this is a bit embarrassing... but I just wrapped up
my personal taxes - and I got a bit of a surprise.
Actually, it was a pretty big, nasty surprise.
And I am going to have to write a great big check and
send it off to the IRS on Tuesday.
I suppose I really should have seen this coming - we
had all kinds of new Real Time subscribers join us in
the last year, and our Trading Update business really
took off. But this tax bill really caught me by surprise.
In any case I need to bring in some cash quickly to pay
this bill.
So bottom line, I am putting RTU on sale for a couple of
days. Call it an "IRS Sale" if you will.
We normally don't discount Trading Update subscriptions...

Tax Sale – Swipe Files
Dateline: 11:45 pm eastern time, 04/14/2003
The market opened higher yesterday, just under the 886.50-
888.50 resistance zone on the SP futures and right at the
1053-1058 resistance zone on the Nasdaq futures. However,
after that early strength the market turned immediately
lower.
The selling gathered momentum after the Employment number
was released. The market headed down for the 880.00-878.50
SP zone and the 1040-1038 Nasdaq zone. Those support areas
stood up... and after lows in those zones, the market turned
up. It wasn't exactly a u-turn... the market slopped around
for a bit... but the bulls eventually put together a rally.
The bounce took us up to make a double top up at 886.50 on
the SP 1053 on the Nasdaq. From there the market turned back
down... and the selling was pretty steady into the close.
In the end, the Dow dropped 58 points, while the SP500 gave
up 5.67 points. The Nasdaq composite dropped 14 points and
the Nasdaq 100 ended 16 points lower.
Well, the bulls just can't seem to put anything together
that sticks. The market has been unable to break 890-892 on
the SP futures and 1059-1060 on the Nasdaq futures. The
bulls will have to put together a convincing breakout over
those zones, and they will have to do it with good breadth
and volume. If they don't, those bears that they beat into
submission last week will come back out with their claws
sharpened. The breadth was not very healthy yesterday, and
the volume was higher on a down day. Until the bulls can
prove themselves by breaking out above resistance (on good
breadth and volume), then we will view this rally as suspect
- and a good shorting opportunity.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Now don't get too excited about the short side... and don't
get married to any of your positions. Remember to obey your
stops, and get out quickly if a trade doesn't go your way
quickly. We like the short side on rallies, but only with
controlled risk.
If the bulls can prove themselves by breaking over
resistance at 890-892 on the SP futures and 1059-1060 on the
Nasdaq futures, then we will flip to the bullish side.
Again, however, don't get emotional... we are not going to
chase any entries. Instead, we will wait for pullbacks to
enter positions.

Real Time Trader's Update
04 / 15 / 2003
.................................................
Dateline: 11:45 pm eastern time, 04/15/2003
The market got off to a big start this week, but then it
struggled. Finally, after fighting resistance at 890 for the
last four days, it broke out higher. After that break the
market rallied strongly and finished the week well. The Dow
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 9 of 14 --

ProductLaunchFormula.com 	© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
Tax Sale – Swipe Files
gained 86 points and the SP500 gained 16 points. Meanwhile,
the Nasdaq composite picked up 38 points, and the Nasdaq 100
gained 36 points.
The rally got the market up over the top of the recent
ranges... that was healthy. The good breadth and volume was
also healthy. Now the bulls just have to be sure they can
hold that hard-fought ground and keep the market over that
old resistance/new support. In addition to the SP and Nasdaq
break over resistance, the Dow came along for the party by
breaking over 8200 zone. In fact, the rally has the Nasdaq
composite close to its February highs.
Of course, not everything is rosy for the market. We are now
again at extreme overbought levels on most of our internal
indicators. And we also have some (a few) bearish
divergences. Although those divergences are pretty minor...
the combination of overbought indicators along with bearish
divergences can be pretty deadly.
So what we have here is a classic battle... a breakout above
strong resistance is bullish, and our indicators are
bearish. That is telling us to be nimble and not overstay
our welcome in any trades.
Look to play both sides tomorrow. We are going to look for
an early dip down to support (past resistance) for a buying
opportunity. But, again, don't overstay your welcome in this
trade.
This market has had a hard time making up its mind... it has
been bouncing back and forth on an almost daily basis. The
moves are coming fast and furious... and they have been in
both directions. Of course, this is exactly what the doctor
ordered for short term traders... and it fits us just fine.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

some tension in the market, and we should see that released
in a sustained move in the not-too-distant future.
